summary: The mcp-server built-in kind — embedded, fields-only, collection address mcpServers.* — with its cited strictest-documented-profile default contract, in the engine std-lib and SDK provider

new.description: The mcp-server kind reads a real `.mcp.json` `mcpServers.*` entry as an mcp-server member; its default contract fires on a broken server config and passes a clean one (fixture mirrors the real Claude Code layout).

edit.description: Add the `mcp-server` kind to the embedded std-lib: embedded locus, fields-only shape, collection address `mcpServers.*`. Its field schema is a fetched-and-cited external fact about the Claude Code MCP-server config format (0014).
The embedded built-in lock gains the mcp-server kind's declaration rows (kind fact + default-contract clauses), derived from the module.
Add the `mcpServer` kind value and `mcpServerDefaultContract` (strictest-documented-profile clauses, each cited + dated), built with the same `kind<T>()` constructor.
Export `mcpServer` and `mcpServerDefaultContract` from the `@dtmd/temper/claude-code` provider subpath.
Teach `governed_by_any` that the mcp-server kind's collection address governs `.mcp.json`; `.mcp.json` is wholly this manifest, so its `coverage.unmodeled-surface` finding retires cleanly.
`embedded_declaration_rows()` fixture gains the mcp-server rows so the embedded lock still byte-equals the SDK module's emit.

tests.asserts: an .mcp.json server reads as an mcp-server member; the shipped default contract fires on the strictest documented profile and passes clean input; the built-in locks stay byte-equal; .mcp.json no longer fires coverage.unmodeled-surface

acceptance: temper models an .mcp.json server as an mcp-server member with its cited default contract; the built-in lock stays byte-equal both sides; .mcp.json no longer fires coverage.unmodeled-surface.
notes: Phase 1 of 0021, now open — HOOK-KIND shipped (76aaa83), which wired the main.rs manifest read dispatch and the src/kind.rs helpers this second manifest kind inherits (no main.rs edit needed). Collection address mcpServers.*. Build fetches + cites the current Claude Code MCP-server config doc (0014) for the field schema and strictest-profile default contract. .mcp.json is wholly this manifest, so its coverage.unmodeled-surface retires cleanly. scoped at 8998b73

summary: Phase 2 of 0021 (manifest write side): SDK member constructors + erasure, canonical whole-manifest emit write, bundle.rs to general-write, guard coverage of represented manifests

gate.reason: Phase 2 write side of 0021, still a placeholder — NOT one gate-sized entry. MANIFEST-ADAPTER-READ shipped (3611335): src/json_manifest.rs is on disk with its read face, so the original entry-refs blocker (write-face file absent) has cleared. HOOK-KIND shipped (76aaa83); phase 1's remaining kind (MCP-SERVER-KIND) has not, and this entry needs decomposing into a real chain before it can be blockedBy. Re-scope into a blockedBy chain (SDK erasure, emit write face, bundle conversion, guard) behind MCP-SERVER-KIND once phase 1 ships; the post-ship reconciliation audit re-tests this condition.

edit.description: Write face of the JSON manifest adapter (extends the phase-1 read face): canonical whole-manifest regeneration — declared collection-address order then the opaque residue, LF — under the container member's provenance row. Registration members carry no lock rows of their own (0012). Phase-1 MANIFEST-ADAPTER-READ creates this file with the read face; this entry adds the write face.

Emit wires the represented-manifest projection: a container member whose segment is a represented manifest regenerates through the general write face, not json_splice (which stays the unrepresented-manifest path).
Convert bundle.rs's bespoke serde_json manifest writes (plugin.json, marketplace.json, hooks.json) to general-write instances of the canonical manifest write (0015's named consequence).
Guard coverage of represented manifests — the PreToolUse guard checks a represented manifest's members against their contract.
settings.json's full coverage.unmodeled-surface retirement: once represented, its permissions/opaque residue is covered, completing the retirement HOOK-KIND began for the hooks segment.
SDK member-authoring constructors for hook/mcp-server registration members and their erasure into the emit payload (the embedded-member facts the write face renders).

acceptance: Deferred until re-scoped. When it opens (behind phase 1): a represented JSON manifest regenerates whole via the canonical write face (declared order then residue, LF), bundle.rs's bespoke writes route through it, the guard covers represented manifests, and settings.json's unmodeled-surface finding fully retires.
notes: Phase 2 of 0021, write side. Placeholder, NOT one gate-sized entry — re-scope into a blockedBy chain (SDK erasure, emit write face, bundle conversion, guard) once phase 1 ships. Parked not blockedBy: entry-refs gate needs edit paths on disk, and src/json_manifest.rs's write face is created by phase-1 MANIFEST-ADAPTER-READ. chain.ts sanctions re-scoping parked entries before they open; reconciliation re-derives it. scoped at 3830cd9

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, public on npm — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP, 543f2fd); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Interim SDK releases ride 0.0.x — sdk/package.json is 0.0.6 (the unpublished 0.1.0 re-cut down at 55d7ee4; 0.1.0 waits for the v0.1 launch tag it gates). Re-verified live at 747f8cc: still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
